text:
Once upon a time, near the edge of the Whispering Woods, there was a tiny cottage that served as an inn for traveling musicians. The innkeeper was a quirky old cat named Felix. Felix had a twinkle in his eye and an unparalleled penchant for jamming tunes late into the night.

On an especially damp autumn eve, a solitary traveler arrived seeking comfort. This traveler, known as Miss Harriet, was a poet with a heart big enough for everyone.

Felix listened carefully to Miss Harriet's tales of far-off lands and melancholy hearts. As the night deepened, Felix began to strum a dusty guitar, his voice lilting through the thin walls.

Miss Harriet found herself enchanted by Felix's melodies. She watched him play, his fingers dancing across the strings as if guided by invisible thread. She couldn't help but weave her own song into the moonlit air.

By the time the stars brightened the gloom, Felix had spun them a roundelay of
